title: Horseback Riding in Greater Austin
subtitle: Trail rides, lesson barns, and equine experiences

popup.title: Greater Austin's Riding Scene
popup.subtitle: 17 public-facing operations within an hour of downtown
popup.body: Greater Austin offers a dense cluster of recreational horseback riding options — from guided ranch trail rides minutes from downtown to structured hunter–jumper lesson barns and wellness-focused equine programs at a luxury resort. Facilities span four corridors: southeast toward Bluff Springs and Lockhart, northeast toward Manor, west toward Bee Cave and Dripping Springs, and south toward Buda and Driftwood.

popup.title: Texas Trail Rides / Lone Star Ranch
popup.subtitle: 8601 Bluff Springs Rd · Southeast Austin
popup.body: The classic "Texas ranch trail ride" experience just south of downtown. Lone Star Ranch offers family-friendly guided horseback trail rides alongside ATV tours, skeet shooting, fishing, and corporate events. For a more immersive ranch experience farther out, Maverick Horseback Riding near Lockhart pairs ecology-focused trail rides with BBQ rides on a large Central Texas ranch about 30–40 minutes away.

popup.title: Southern Trails
popup.subtitle: 15701 Decker Lake Rd · Manor
popup.body: Just 15 minutes from downtown, Southern Trails runs highly rated guided ranch rides on a 100–120-acre property with scenic terrain. Kids' "Little Dude" rides and sunset trail options make it accessible to all ages. Nearby on Cameron Road, Manor Equestrian Center offers family-friendly lessons, boarding, and summer camps, while White Fences Equestrian Center provides boutique dressage and hunter/jumper instruction for committed riders.

popup.title: Bee Cave Riding Center
popup.subtitle: 15740 Hamilton Pool Rd · Bee Cave
popup.body: Hill Country facility offering hunter/jumper, dressage, and western instruction for all ages and levels. Further along the western corridor, Rio Vista Farm in Dripping Springs is one of Austin's longest-running English barns — accepting riders without their own horses for lessons, camps, and boarding. Coraggio Equestrian in southwest Austin adds 100+ acres of shaded Hill Country trails and personalized training. Lone Star Riding Club in Driftwood serves the entire region as a US Pony Club Riding Center.

popup.title: Miraval Austin Resort & Spa
popup.subtitle: Northwest Austin · Lake Travis
popup.body: Overlooking Lake Travis on 220+ acres, Miraval Austin integrates horseback riding into a holistic wellness retreat — with rhythmic riding, equine-assisted self-leadership experiences, and private trail rides as part of all-inclusive overnight or day-pass packages. In north Austin, Tri-Star Farm (Spicewood Springs Rd) and Switch Willo Stables — a long-running hunter/jumper show barn since the 1960s — serve riders seeking structured lessons and youth camp programs.

popup.title: Find your ride
popup.subtitle: Read the full research report
popup.body: All 17 operators are profiled in depth — with guidance on choosing between one-time trail rides and recurring lessons, English vs. Western focus, distance from central Austin, and age and weight restrictions to check before booking.
